Q: Can I solve this? A: Yes, straightforward multiplication. confidence 0.9.
<|/meta|>

James runs 3 sprints each time, and each sprint is 60 meters.

So each running session is:
3 × 60 = 180 meters

He does this 3 times a week:
180 × 3 = 540 meters

<|meta|>
Q: Final verification. A: 3 sprints/session × 60 m = 180 m/session, and 180 × 3 = 540 m/week. confidence 0.95.
<|/meta|>

\boxed{540}<|im_end|>

Q: Can I solve this? A: Yes, this is a straightforward word problem. Confidence about 0.7.

Each chicken gets 3 cups of feed per day total.

So for 20 chickens, the total feed needed per day is:
\[
20 \times 3 = 60 \text{ cups}

15 + 25 = 40 \text{ cups}
\]

So the final meal needs:
\[
60 - 40 = 20 \text{ cups}
